name: Pico de la Miel y Cancho Gordo – circular desde La Cabrera
region_intro: Hiking around La Cabrera provides access to a distinctive mountainous topography, characterized by a magnificent granite massif of Palaeozoic origin. The region, part of the Sierra Norte de Madrid, features prominent peaks like Cancho Gordo (1,564 m) and Pico del Miel (1,392 m), which offer challenging ascents and panoramic views. Natural springs and streams, alongside diverse flora including Pyrenean Oak and Holm Oak, contribute to the varied landscapes found on its trails. Best hiking trails around La Cabrera The most popular hiking route is Pico de la Miel y Cancho Gordo – circular desde La Cabrera, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views from two of the region's highest peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico de la Miel – Cancho Gordo (1,563 m) loop from La Cabrera, a difficult 9.1 miles (14.7 km) path. This trail navigates rugged granite formations and provides extensive vistas across the Sierra de la Cabrera. Local hikers also love the Cairn and Trail Marker – Pico de la Miel loop from La Cabrera, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards Pico de la Miel, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around La Cabrera is defined by granite massifs, prominent peaks, and diverse woodlands, offering routes for various ability levels. The routes in La Cabrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore La Cabrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Cabrera?
A: La Cabrera offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 20 trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy walks to more demanding ascents, allowing you to explore the region's unique granite landscapes and natural beauty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in La Cabrera?
A: The terrain in La Cabrera is primarily characterized by a magnificent granite massif of Palaeozoic origin. You'll encounter rugged rocky paths, especially on routes leading to prominent peaks like Pico del Miel and Cancho Gordo. The southern slopes also feature diverse woodlands with Pyrenean Oak, Holm Oak, Poplar, and Willow, offering varied and picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, La Cabrera has trails suitable for beginners and families. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 2 easy trails available. For a moderate option that is not too long, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40376000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viacrucis Cross – Cross on the Boulder loop from La Cabrera&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 5 km.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced hikers in La Cabrera?
A: Experienced hikers looking for a challenge will find several demanding routes. The region is known for its challenging ascents to prominent peaks. A popular difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957089156/pico-de-la-miel-y-cancho-gordo-circular-desde-la-cabrera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Miel y Cancho Gordo – circular desde La Cabrera&lt;/a&gt;, a 11.6 km trail offering expansive views from two of the region's highest points. Another challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1290418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Miel – Cancho Gordo (1,563 m) loop from La Cabrera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.7 km and navigates rugged granite formations.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in La Cabrera?
A: Yes, La Cabrera offers several circular hiking routes that allow you to experience the region's dramatic rocky walls and impressive vistas. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957089156/pico-de-la-miel-y-cancho-gordo-circular-desde-la-cabrera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Miel y Cancho Gordo – circular desde La Cabrera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/991896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cairn and Trail Marker – Pico de la Miel loop from La Cabrera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in La Cabrera?
A: While hiking in La Cabrera, you'll encounter a striking granite massif and prominent peaks. Notable summits include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2876509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Gordo (1,563 m)&lt;/a&gt; and Pico del Miel, both offering panoramic views. You might also discover natural springs and streams, such as the Alfrecho stream. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Cabrera?
A: La Cabrera offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, so early morning hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but higher elevations may present colder conditions and require appropriate gear.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trails in La Cabrera?
A: La Cabrera is located just 56 km (35 mi) from Madrid, making it accessible. While specific bus routes to trailheads may vary, public transport options from Madrid to La Cabrera municipality are generally available. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most current information on reaching the area.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in La Cabrera?
A: Parking is typically available within or near the municipality of La Cabrera, serving as a common starting point for many trails. Specific parking areas may be designated near popular trailheads, especially for routes leading to Pico del Miel or Cancho Gordo. Arriving early, particularly on weekends, is recommended to secure a spot.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Cabrera?
A: The hiking trails in La Cabrera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the challenging ascents, the spectacular panoramic views from the peaks, and the unique granite landscapes that define the region. Many appreciate the opportunity to disconnect and enjoy the natural charm of the Sierra Norte de Madrid.

Q: Are there any specific peaks I should aim to reach for panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. La Cabrera is home to several significant summits renowned for their panoramic views. &lt;strong&gt;Pico del Miel (1,392 m)&lt;/strong&gt; is highly popular for its challenging ascent and spectacular vistas over the Sierra de la Cabrera. &lt;strong&gt;Cancho Gordo (1,564 m)&lt;/strong&gt; is another characteristic peak offering expansive views. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957089156/pico-de-la-miel-y-cancho-gordo-circular-desde-la-cabrera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Miel y Cancho Gordo – circular desde La Cabrera&lt;/a&gt;, combine these prominent peaks.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in La Cabrera?
A: Generally, hiking with dogs is permitted on many trails in natural areas like La Cabrera. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as natural water sources may not always be reliable.
tips: The peak of La Miel is a classic among hikers and climbers who visit Madrid. A short but challenging climb if you start in La Cabrera and when you reach the top you will only have to sit down to enjoy the incredible panoramic views at 1,392 meters above sea level. From here you can then follow the path until you reach Cancho Gordo, one of the highest peaks in the area.

Rock formation within the Cabrera granite massif with spectacular views. To access its top, you must first climb a steep path to a small hill and then climb a section and reach the geodesic vertex and get the reward of the impressive views.
